terms.definition: A measurable or specified property whose value can be expressed as a numerical value multiplied by a unit.
The number multiplying a unit when a quantity is expressed in that unit.
A ratio of equal quantities used as a multiplicative factor of one to change unit representation without changing the quantity.
A description of a quantity type in terms of base quantity types such as length, time, and mass, independent of a particular unit.
The requirement that quantities added or equated have compatible dimensions; satisfying it is necessary but does not prove a model is correct.
A unit formed by multiplying, dividing, or exponentiating other units, such as metres per second or square centimetres.
